description_long: This object consists of four distinct knives or swords, each with unique blade shapes and handle designs. The first blade on the left features a wavy, flame-like edge and a rounded guard, while the second has a straight, double-edged blade with a prominent circular pommel and a matching sheath. The third blade is a classic straight sword with a wide blade and a rounded pommel, and the fourth is a curved, single-edged blade with a hooked guard and a dark handle. The materials appear to be primarily dark metal for the blades and guards, with some wooden or dark composite elements in the handles. The overall finish is matte to semi-glossy, with minimal ornamentation and a robust, utilitarian appearance. These weapons are likely intended for display, collection, or theatrical use rather than practical combat.
description: A set of four visually distinct knives or swords, each with unique blade shapes and dark, robust handles, featuring both straight and curved designs.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four blades side by side, highlighting their unique shapes and handle designs.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profiles of the blades, emphasizing their sharpness and the varying thickness of each weapon.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the backs of the blades and handles, with the sheath visible on the second sword.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profiles and the alignment of the handles and guards.
attribute_blade_type: wavy double-edged, straight double-edged, straight single-edged, curved single-edged
attribute_handle_design: rounded and contoured handles, some with dark wood or composite material, one with a hooked guard, all with prominent pommels
